content: Hey, I'm planning a ski trip with my wife to Park City next month. Any tips for the best runs?
Park City is fantastic! Are you experienced skiers or is this more of a beginner trip?
We're intermediate. I've been skiing since I was a kid, but my wife is newer to it. We both love the powder days though.
For intermediates, I'd recommend Deer Valley's blue runs - they're well-groomed and less crowded. Have you been to Utah before?
Yeah, we went to Snowbird last year. That was intense! I'm a DevOps Engineer at a cloud platform company, so I can work remotely while we're there if needed.
That's perfect for a ski trip! Being able to work remotely gives you flexibility. What's your favorite part about skiing?
The quiet on the mountain early in the morning. And I love how it's both challenging and meditative. Speaking of tech, I've been working on some home automation projects with Rust programming.
That's an interesting combo - skiing and Rust programming! What kind of home automation are you building?
Mostly smart lighting and temperature controls. I've got this whole system where I can adjust everything from my phone. Oh, and I recently adopted a parrot named Milo from a shelter.
A parrot named Milo sounds wonderful! Does he travel with you or stay home when you're on ski trips?
He stays with my neighbor. I've got a 55-gallon aquarium too that I'm really proud of - it's got some rare tropical fish I've collected over the years.
That's quite the setup you have at home! Between the aquarium, parrot, and automation projects, you're keeping busy. Any specific restaurants you're looking forward to in Park City?
We're thinking about trying that new farm-to-table place everyone's been talking about. I'm also hoping to check out some local breweries after a day on the slopes.
Sounds like a perfect trip! The farm-to-table scene in Park City is really growing. Since you mentioned working remotely, do you find it hard to disconnect from work during vacations?

Sometimes. Being a DevOps Engineer means I'm always on call to some degree. But I've gotten better at setting boundaries. Last year I missed a major incident because I was on a black run at Snowbird - worth it!
That's the spirit! A little adventure is worth the occasional work trade-off. Anything else you're planning for your Park City trip?
We're thinking about taking a day to explore Salt Lake City too. My wife wants to check out some art galleries there. Oh, and I need to make sure Milo's cage is extra secure before we leave!
Salt Lake City has a great art scene! And smart thinking about Milo's cage. Have a wonderful trip, and enjoy those Utah slopes!
